item.title: Hedge Fund SPX Capital Faces Shake-Up, Moves to Exit London
item.description: Brazilian hedge fund SPX Capital is undergoing a major restructuring, with senior partners departing and a rethink of operations that will include shutting its London office, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Korea Surpasses Canada as World’s Seventh-Largest Stock Market
item.description: South Korea’s equity market has overtaken Canada’s as the world’s seventh largest, propelled by insatiable demand for chips powering artificial intelligence.

item.title: Montage Tops CATL as Priciest Dual-Listed Stock After Chip Rally
item.description: Montage Technology Co. has overtaken Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. as the most expensive dual-listed stock in Hong Kong relative to its mainland shares, propelled by surging demand for AI chips.

item.title: RBNZ’s Frequent Remit Adjustments Risk Policy Error, OECD Warns
item.description: New Zealand should reconsider how frequently it adjusts the Reserve Bank’s remit because it raises the risk of monetary policy mistakes, according to the Organisation for Economic Cooperation and Development.

item.title: Milei Ally Demands Answers to Graft Allegations Against Top Aide
item.description: Argentine President Javier Milei’s cabinet chief was urged to disclose his finances and real estate assets by the libertarian leader’s senior ally in Congress, in the first public sign of friction within the ruling coalition over a growing corruption scandal.

item.title: Japan Has All Fronts in Sight in Response to Yen Speculation
item.description: Japan is prepared to respond on all fronts to speculative moves in the foreign exchange market, according to the nation’s top currency official, following suspected intervention over the holiday period.

item.title: Wall Street Dares to Ask If China’s Property Turnaround Is Close
item.description: Just months into China’s housing downturn, the nation’s second-biggest developer declared the worst is over. A year later, its chairman backtracked on the call, and by 2025, it was teetering on the brink of a default.

item.title: Anthropic Is Making Its Claude Chatbot More Appealing to Consumers
item.description: Artificial intelligence startup Anthropic PBC, which originally aimed its Claude chatbot at businesses, is looking to capitalize on recent inroads with consumers by making the software more appealing to everyday people.

item.title: Surveillance Firm Hawkeye 360 Said to Raise $416 Million in IPO
item.description: Hawkeye 360 Inc., a provider of satellite-based signals intelligence for US government agencies, is raising $416 million in a US initial public offering priced at the top of a marketed range, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Chinese Creditors Turn to Hong Kong Courts to Enforce Rulings
item.description: Chinese creditors who have struggled to recover funds from distressed property developers on the mainland are increasingly turning to Hong Kong courts in their quest for repayment.

item.title: GameStop’s Cohen Says He’s Selling on eBay to Help Fund Takeover
item.description: GameStop Corp. Chief Executive Officer Ryan Cohen is raising money to help pay for his proposed $56 billion takeover of eBay Inc., one pair of socks at a time.

item.title: UOB Profit Dips on Lending Income, CEO Says Uncertainty Elevated
item.description: United Overseas Bank Ltd. first-quarter profit dropped as lending and fee income both fell.

item.title: Hormuz Crisis Shows Gaps in Taiwan’s High-Tech ‘Silicon Shield’
item.description: Dominance in chip manufacturing has made it prosperous — and highly vulnerable.

item.title: Debt Funds Close in on Banks as Biggest UK Real Estate Lenders
item.description: Debt funds have doubled their share of the UK real estate lending market over the last five years as banks, constrained by post-crisis regulations, have lost ground in the direct lending market.

item.title: Malaysia Set to Hold Rate as Energy Crisis Yet to Lift Inflation
item.description: Malaysia’s central bank will likely keep its benchmark interest rate unchanged as inflation remains benign despite higher global oil prices due to the Iran war.

item.title: China Bond Futures Liberalization Spurs Calls for Wider Access
item.description: China’s decision to allow a select group of global investors to trade bond futures has prompted demands for deeper reforms that may boost the appeal of the world’s second-largest debt market.

item.title: Taiwan’s $286 Billion Pension Fund Trims Its Dollar Exposure
item.description: Taiwan’s largest pension fund has reduced some of its US currency exposure amid heightened market volatility and a broader global reassessment of dollar assets.

item.title: Singapore Parliament Pledges No Jobless Growth in AI Era, CNA Reports
item.description: Singapore’s lawmakers unanimously passed a motion affirming that the country will not have jobless growth amid the artificial intelligence transition, CNA reported.

item.title: Japan’s Stocks Set to Jump on US Tech Rally, Iran Deal Optimism
item.description: Japanese stocks are set to surge as markets reopen from holidays, as investors catch up with the global equities rally driven by optimism the US and Iran were nearing a deal to end their conflict.

item.title: Gold Holds Gain as US-Iran Truce Hopes Ease Inflation Concerns
item.description: Gold steadied after its biggest daily advance since end-March, as hopes of a US-Iran deal sent oil prices plunging and eased inflation concerns.

item.title: Anxious Australian Retirees Ramp Up Pension Fund Withdrawals
item.description: Australian retirees are withdrawing their pension savings at increasing rates as global turmoil and surging inflation erodes their already fragile confidence.

item.title: Japan to Return With Traders Optimistic on Iran: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian equities were set to rise Thursday, tracking record highs on Wall Street, as optimism grew that the US and Iran were nearing a deal to end the conflict that had rattled markets and clouded the economic outlook.

item.title: Oil Steadies as the US and Iran Weigh Fresh Proposal to End War
item.description: Oil steadied after plunging 7% in the previous session, as the US and Iran weighed a fresh proposal to end the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Fertilizer Makers See Earnings Windfall as War Disrupts Supplies
item.description: Fertilizer makers CF Industries Holdings Inc. and Nutrien Ltd. each reported nearly 20% jumps in sales for the latest quarter, showing the extent of the Iran war’s upheaval on supply chains for the key crop nutrients.

item.title: China’s Midea Group Raises $2.2 Billion in Convertible Bond Sale
item.description: Chinese appliance maker Midea Group Co. raised $2.2 billion from the upsized sale of dual-tranche convertible bonds with zero coupon, according to terms of the deal seen by Bloomberg News.

item.title: Fortinet Jumps on Outlook Hike, Billings Growth Spurt
item.description: Fortinet Inc. shares rose as much as 24% in post-market trading after the cybersecurity company raised its full-year revenue outlook above expectations, following its largest billings expansion in over three years.

item.title: Albemarle Shares Jump as Lithium Recovery Delivers Earnings Beat
item.description: Albemarle Corp. shares surged after the lithium producer delivered better-than-expected quarterly results as higher prices and stronger volume lifted sales and earnings.

item.title: Blue Owl BDCs Buy Back $85 Million of Shares as Loan Values Sink
item.description: Two private credit funds managed by Blue Owl Capital Inc. bought back $85 million of shares as volatility in technology markets and a selloff in publicly traded loans brought down their value.

item.title: Zillow Shares Drop As Profit Outlook Miss Overshadows Upbeat 1Q
item.description: Zillow Group Inc. shares dropped 8% in post-market trading on Wednesday after the company’s second-quarter profit forecast missed Wall Street expectations, overshadowing upbeat results for the first quarter of the year.

item.title: Musk Weighed Offering Altman Tesla Board Seat, OpenAI Jury Told
item.description: Elon Musk at one point considered recruiting Sam Altman to serve on Tesla Inc.’s board of directors, jurors were told Wednesday at the trial over the feud between the two titans of artificial intelligence.
